---
title: "Tracy's Beaksedge (Rhynchospora tracyi)"
slug: "rhynchospora-tracyi"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/257756052/medium.jpeg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Tracy's Beaksedge"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Cyperaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Cyperaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/257756052/medium.jpeg" alt="Tracy's Beaksedge" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/mmcmillian">
mmcmillian</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/149570085">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Cyperaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=167907)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Rare species in Florida found in moist to wet habitats.

## Natural history notes

A grass-like wetland plant characterized by its slender stems and clusters of spikelets with distinctive bristled fruits. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as flatwoods, bogs, and seepage areas, often in sandy soils. It grows during the warmer months and contributes to vegetation structure and diversity in wetland communities.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/167907-Rhynchospora-tracyi)
-   [Local profile](https://plant-directory.ifas.ufl.edu/plant-directory/rhynchospora-tracyi/)
